data.title: SUI set to hit $2.07-$2.42 by Feb 2026. Currently trades at $1.81 with RSI 59.33. Bulls need to hold $1.75 support.
data.description: The stock of SUI is currently valued at $1.81, with a neutral RSI of 59.33. Analysts predict a potential breakout to a range between $2.07 and $2.42 based on technical indicators, provided that the bulls can uphold support levels at $1.75 throughout February. This indicates a potential increase in value for SUI, signaling an opportunity for growth in the coming weeks.

data.title: Aon Global Limited to redeem and delist 2.875% Senior Notes due 2026 from NYSE.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, in London, Aon Global Limited, formerly known as Aon plc, a subsidiary of Aon plc (NYSE:AON), revealed plans to fully redeem and remove its 2.875% Senior Notes that mature in 2026 from the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: DataMesh launches DataMesh Robotics for industrial AI training with executable digital twins in Singapore on Jan. 15, 2026.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, in Singapore, a prominent digital twin and spatial intelligence technology provider named DataMesh unveiled DataMesh Robotics. This new product offers an embodied AI data solution tailored for industrial and facilities settings. It operates on an Executable Industrial platform and aims to enhance operational efficiency and automation in such environments.

data.title: Four startups win UAE FoodTech Challenge, sharing $2M to pilot solutions in UAE and Global South; to support food security.
data.description: During Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week, a global agri-food innovation competition announced four winning startups. HyveGeo, Permia Sensing, Flybox, and Akorn Technology were selected to receive a total of USD 2 million to pilot and scale their solutions in the UAE and the Global South. The awards were given on January 15, 2026, in Abu Dhabi, UAE.

data.title: Yum China will report Q4 and fiscal year 2025 financial results on Jan 15, 2026 before US market opens.
data.description: Yum China Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: YUMC and HKEX: 9987) based in Shanghai disclosed the upcoming release of its unaudited financial results for Q4 and fiscal year 2025. The announcement states that the Company will unveil these results before the U.S. market commences trading.

data.title: Markel Insurance promotes Ben House to Director of Energy & Power, overseeing Renewable Energy.
data.description: London, January 15, 2026 - Markel Insurance, a subsidiary of Markel Group Inc. (NYSE: MKL), has appointed Ben House as the Director of Energy & Power. House will now be responsible for managing Markel's Renewable Energy division. This promotion is effective immediately.

data.title: AI investments rising, CEOs taking control. Companies to double AI spending by 2026, 94% bullish on ROI. CEOs leading AI strategy.
data.description: A recent report by BCG reveals that companies are looking to significantly increase their investment in artificial intelligence (AI), with plans to double spending on AI by 2026. The study indicates that 94% of companies are optimistic about the return on investment (ROI) from AI initiatives. Surprisingly, even if immediate returns are not evident, 94% of companies intend to sustain their investment in AI. The report highlights a shift in AI strategy decision-making, with 72% of CEOs now leading the charge. Additionally, half of the companies surveyed are focusing on enhancing employee skills in AI technologies, indicating a growing emphasis on workforce development in this space.

data.title: UK retailers facing £27 billion return burden can combat it using Rich Communication Services and WhatsApp.
data.description: In December, UK retailers experienced a surge in spending; however, January brings the burden of returns. Infobip, a global cloud communications platform, emphasizes the importance of Rich Communication Services (RCS) and WhatsApp for retailers to address this issue. Retail Economics data forecasts that annual returns in the UK retail sector could surpass £27 billion. Returns pose a substantial financial impact on retailers, highlighting the need for effective solutions like RCS and WhatsApp to manage the growing challenge.

data.title: UK retailers face rising fraud, with 48% considering closure or scaling back in 2026, as per payabl. research.
data.description: In London, UK retailers are facing increasing challenges due to rising fraud, as highlighted in a recent study. The research conducted by payabl., a top European financial technology provider, reveals that half of these businesses are in jeopardy of either shutting down or reducing their operations in the upcoming year. The "Fraud in Europe" report indicates that fraud is severely impacting retailers in terms of operations, profit margins, and customer confidence. An alarming 48% of retail executives have contemplated downsizing or closing their establishments in response to these challenges.

data.title: OPEX Corp wins two awards for its Falcon+ imaging solution at 2025 DM Awards ceremony in London.
data.description: In Moorestown, New Jersey, OPEX® Corporation, known worldwide for cutting-edge automation solutions in warehouse, document, and mail processing, has received accolades from Document Manager magazine. The prestigious DM Awards in 2025, hosted in London, saw OPEX clinching victory in two significant categories. The Falcon+® document imaging solution by OPEX triumphed as the Imaging Product of the Year: High Volume and also secured the Hardware Product of the Year award. Additionally, OPEX received recognition as runners-up in other categories, showcasing their excellence in the industry.
